dialogue.utterance: Unit 3, respond for a reported seizure at a restaurant in [CITY].
Hi, I'm Tessa, an EMT with the rescue squad. What brought you to call 911 today?
He had a seizure about 40 minutes ago while in the kitchen. Hit his left shoulder when he fell.
Sir, can you open your eyes for us?
Nice, you opened your eyes spontaneously. Can you tell me your name?
Uh... Michael.
Good. Can you squeeze my finger with your right hand?
He obeyed commands. That's a motor 6, verbal 4, eye 4. GCS of 14, consistent with post-ictal, with possible stupor initially.
Sir, I can see your airway is clear and patent. No sounds of obstruction present.
Breathing appears normal with equal chest movement and no signs of shortness of breath.
Take a deep breath for me so I can check your breath sounds.
Respirations are 18 per minute, equal and unlabored. Measured again as 16 later.
His skin looks pink, warm, and dry. Radial pulse is strong. Initially the pulse showed as 0, but it stabilized quickly.
Pulse is 82, regular and strong initially. First oximetry read was 0% - likely due to technical issues.
Blood pressure is 125 over 68. Initially showed as 0/0 due to likely sensor error but quickly stabilized.
Pulse oximeter initially showed 95%, first reading 0% possibly due to perfusion issues during patient boarding.
Michael, can you tell me where you are?
I think I was in the kitchen?
You know your name. Can you tell me the date today?
June 7th, maybe? I wasn't really sure at first. I had a bit of a foggy head like a stupor. Now, it's clearing up.
What happened here, sir?
He was working when he started seizing. No prior history of seizures.
He was lowered to the ground by another employee. No head trauma or vomiting observed.
How would you rate the pain in your shoulder, 0-10?
About a 6.
Does the pain go anywhere else?
No, only in the left shoulder.
Checking skin - no rashes, lesions, bleeding, or swelling noted. Skin is pink, warm, and dry.
Let me check your pupils. Shine a light into each eye.
Pupils 4 mm, equal, round, and reactive. No JVD found in jugular vein assessment.
Can you take a deep breath while I listen to your lungs?
Breath sounds clear and equal bilaterally.
Your abdomen is soft, not tender, and not distended.
No chest wall tenderness or paradoxical movement found. Pulses intact throughout.
Pain in the left shoulder noted, no visible deformity or angulation reported. Pulses are normal.
Any sweating or fever since the event?
No, I haven't been sweating or have a fever.
Did you feel confused after the seizure?
A bit dazed at first, now I feel more like myself.
Any known allergies to any drugs, foods, or other substances?
No known drug allergies.
Any medical conditions or surgery history?
Yes, I have ADHD.
Are you on any medications?
Dexedrine for ADHD.
Attaching you to an EKG for continuous monitoring.
EKG monitoring initiated. Sinus rhythm observed and stable throughout the session.
Let me check your blood glucose.
Blood sugar is 98. In the normal range.
Applying a four-lead electrode monitoring now.
Sinus rhythm ongoing. No arrhythmias or ectopy seen.
We are placing an IV in your left forearm now in case further fluids or meds are needed.
IV placed successfully into the left forearm without complications.
Were you aware of your surroundings when we first found you, Michael?
I think I was a bit confused at first. But now I feel more clear.
You were initially A&Ox4 - oriented to person, time, and self, but not to place or event.
Yeah, everything came back to me after a while.
Any head injury when the incident happened?
No, just the shoulder.
Based on the situation, we'll follow the medical seizure protocol for transport to [COMPANY_NAME].
Repeat pulse is 87 per minute, BP is 120/71. SpO2 has improved to 96%.
Glucose reading again showed as zero. Retrying is necessary.
Pulse 74, BP 107/57, SpO2 97%. Vitals have remained stable.
Transport is appropriate. We are heading to [COMPANY_NAME] for further seizure investigation.
He's alert and oriented now. Follows verbal commands well. Checks out for transfers.
Using a stair chair to safely assist him outside.
Notifying the ER at [COMPANY_NAME] to prepare for his arrival and condition.
Moved to the stretcher and safely secured for ambulance transfer.
All systems go for transport. Ready to depart.
[COMPANY_NAME], we are en route. Patient is A&Ox4, IV in place, and vitals stable.
Blood pressure initially showed as 0/0, now is 107/57 with no head injury or nausea.
Consent and documentation complete. The patient is comfortable and signs EMS released.
Confusion noted initially, but he is A&Ox4 now. Pupils and allergic responses normal.
Direction to [COMPANY_NAME] confirmed. Transport status non-urgent based on mental stability.
Patient is currently conscious and clear, communicating well throughout.
Transport prep complete. Low risk but monitoring for any seizure-related changes.
Using a secure method to lift him onto the stretcher. Capacity and cooperation noted.
Maneuver into a lateral position in the ambulance now that he is stable.
Final checks done. He's breathing well and in stable condition for transport.
No breathing issues, and his status aligns with green transport classification.
BP is 107 over 57, pulse 74, SpO2 at 97%. Vitals stable prior to transport.
Respiratory rate 18, SpO2 still above 95%. All clear for transfer.
How is the shoulder pain now?
Still a 6 out of 10. Manageable but still there.
He was easy to transport and stayed fully conscious and responsive throughout.
Patient handed over to ER. No further EMS protocol indicated at this time.
inded patient as NKDA. Transport procedure and IV maintenance comply.
Patient current under GCS 15 and communicating well.
Standard EKG and IV per protocol have been applied. Blood sugar read once as zero, but no trend is seen.
Using a secure ambulation approach for stretcher transition. Safe and capably cooperating.
Lateral positioning complete in the ambulance now.
All procedures and prior res assessed. Ambulance now on route to [COMPANY_NAME].
All aboard and level. We believe we are on track for full evaluation as normal.
Stable speed maintained in transit with procedures under control.
Handoff to ER complete. Squad returning to service as SOP.
All details documented and handed over, equipment secured and clean, otherwise clear.
On course and steady. All aboard safe for facility arrival.
Patient accepted transport conditions and signed CMS. Travel billed through [COMPANY_NAME].
Medical protocol followed, and transport went smoothly per evaluation.
Continued safe transport with all monitoring intact.
No additional complications. Baseline stable or improving. ER should handle without incident.
No alarms active. Patient ready for checkup upon arrival.
ER advised to monitor for any seizure sequelae or status changes.
Non-critical status confirmed. Full records forwarded for ER intake.
Doors exit confirmed. Arrival imminent.
Transport and arrival procedures successful and under protocol.
ARRIVAL confirmed at [COMPANY_NAME].
